name: Capture Parent/Child Issue Relationships in Worktree Add

userStories.title: Expand Parent struct in jiraRawResponse
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Parent field in jiraRawResponse.Fields to capture full issue details (id, key, summary, status, priority, issuetype) so that parent information matches the LinkedIssue structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Parent struct includes ID field
Parent struct includes Key field
Parent struct includes nested Fields with Summary, Status.Name, Priority.Name, IssueType.Name
Existing parent key parsing still works

userStories.title: Add Subtasks field to jiraRawResponse
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Subtasks field added to jiraRawResponse.Fields to capture child issues from the JIRA API response.
acceptanceCriteria: Subtasks field is a slice of structs matching Parent structure
Each subtask includes ID, Key, and nested Fields (Summary, Status, Priority, IssueType)
Empty subtasks array parses correctly

userStories.title: Add Parent and Children fields to JiraTicketDetails
userStories.description: As a developer, I want JiraTicketDetails to have Parent (*LinkedIssue) and Children ([]LinkedIssue) fields so that the processed issue data includes parent/child relationships.
acceptanceCriteria: JiraTicketDetails.Parent is of type *LinkedIssue
JiraTicketDetails.Children is of type []LinkedIssue
Both fields are nil/empty when issue has no parent/children

userStories.title: Convert raw parent to LinkedIssue during parsing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the service to convert the raw parent response to a LinkedIssue struct when parsing issue data.
acceptanceCriteria: Parent is converted to LinkedIssue with ID, Key, Summary, Status, Priority, IssueType
Nil parent in raw response results in nil Parent in JiraTicketDetails
Parent conversion handles missing optional fields gracefully

userStories.title: Convert raw subtasks to Children LinkedIssues during parsing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the service to convert the raw subtasks array to Children []LinkedIssue when parsing issue data.
acceptanceCriteria: Each subtask is converted to LinkedIssue with ID, Key, Summary, Status, Priority, IssueType
Empty subtasks array results in empty Children slice
Subtask conversion handles missing optional fields gracefully

userStories.title: Recursive parent fetching with depth limit
userStories.description: As a developer, I want parent issues to be fetched recursively following the same MaxDepth configuration used for linked issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Parent is fetched recursively when depth < MaxDepth
Parent key is added to lookup table before fetching
Parent fetching stops when MaxDepth is reached
Parent's linked issues are also fetched following MaxDepth rules

userStories.title: Recursive child fetching with depth limit
userStories.description: As a developer, I want child issues to be fetched recursively following the same MaxDepth configuration used for linked issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Children are fetched recursively when depth < MaxDepth
Each child key is added to lookup table before fetching
Child fetching stops when MaxDepth is reached
Child's linked issues are also fetched following MaxDepth rules

userStories.title: Shared lookup table for deduplication
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a shared lookup table across parent, child, and linked issue fetching to prevent duplicate fetches and infinite loops.
acceptanceCriteria: Single lookup table is used for all issue types (parent, child, linked)
Issues already in lookup table are not fetched again
Circular references (A parent of B, B linked to A) do not cause infinite loops
Lookup table is keyed by issue key (e.g., 'PROJ-123')

userStories.title: Generate Parent section in markdown
userStories.description: As a user, I want the generated markdown files to include a Parent section when the issue has a parent, formatted as '- **Parent:** KEY - Summary'.
acceptanceCriteria: Parent section appears after issue details and before Children section
Parent is formatted as '- **Parent:** KEY - Summary'
Parent section is omitted when issue has no parent
Section header is '## Parent'

userStories.title: Generate Children section in markdown
userStories.description: As a user, I want the generated markdown files to include a Children section when the issue has children, formatted as '- **is parent of:** KEY - Summary'.
acceptanceCriteria: Children section appears after Parent section and before Linked Issues
Each child is formatted as '- **is parent of:** KEY - Summary'
Children section is omitted when issue has no children
Section header is '## Children'

userStories.title: Deduplicate parent from linked issues
userStories.description: As a user, I want issues that appear as both parent AND linked issue to show only in the Parent section, not in Linked Issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Parent issue is removed from linked issues list if present
Parent appears only in Parent section
Other linked issues are unaffected

userStories.title: Deduplicate children from linked issues
userStories.description: As a user, I want issues that appear as both child AND linked issue to show only in the Children section, not in Linked Issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Child issues are removed from linked issues list if present
Children appear only in Children section
Other linked issues are unaffected

userStories.title: Unit tests for parent/child data model changes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests covering the new Parent and Children fields in both raw response parsing and JiraTicketDetails.
acceptanceCriteria: Test parsing of parent field with full details
Test parsing of subtasks array
Test conversion to LinkedIssue for parent
Test conversion to LinkedIssue for children
Test nil/empty cases

userStories.title: Unit tests for recursive fetching and deduplication
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests covering recursive parent/child fetching with depth limits and deduplication.
acceptanceCriteria: Test parent fetching respects MaxDepth
Test child fetching respects MaxDepth
Test lookup table prevents duplicate fetches
Test circular reference handling
Test deep hierarchy exceeding MaxDepth

userStories.title: Unit tests for markdown generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests covering Parent and Children sections in generated markdown.
acceptanceCriteria: Test Parent section format and placement
Test Children section format and placement
Test deduplication from linked issues
Test section ordering (Parent before Children before Linked Issues)
Test omission when no parent/children exist
